title: Lesson Quiz: Overcoming Barriers and Adjusting Plans
description: Assessment of the concept that Problem-solving and adaptability.

questions.prompt: What is a barrier in a plan?

choices.text: A challenge that makes progress more difficult
choices.feedback: Correct. A barrier is something that makes progress harder.

choices.text: A reward for completing a goal
choices.feedback: A barrier is a challenge, not a reward.
A final career choice is not a barrier.

choices.text: A class schedule with no goals attached
choices.feedback: A schedule is not automatically a barrier.

questions.prompt: Adaptability means adjusting when plans or conditions change.

choices.feedback: Adaptability means being able to adjust when change happens.
Correct. Adaptability is the ability to adjust when plans or conditions change.

questions.prompt: Which example best shows problem-solving in planning?

choices.text: Giving up immediately when a challenge appears
Ignoring the barrier and hoping it goes away
Thinking of a new step or adjustment when a barrier appears
choices.feedback: Correct. Problem-solving means identifying a response when a challenge affects a plan.

choices.text: Refusing to make any change to a plan
choices.feedback: Flexibility is often necessary when a barrier appears.

questions.prompt: Why is it important to adjust a plan when needed?

choices.text: Because adjustments can help a student continue making progress
choices.feedback: This is close, but the strongest answer here is the one that most directly explains how adjustment supports continued progress.

choices.text: Because change always means the original goal was a mistake
choices.feedback: A plan may change even when the original goal still matters.

choices.text: Because every plan should be restarted from the beginning
choices.feedback: Students often adjust a plan rather than start over completely.

choices.text: Because adjusting a plan can help a student respond to challenges and keep moving forward
choices.feedback: Correct. Adjustments help students keep making progress when conditions change.

questions.prompt: Changing a plan always means the student has failed.

choices.feedback: Changing a plan does not automatically mean failure. It can show problem-solving and adaptability.
Correct. Adjusting a plan can be a responsible way to respond to barriers.
